Question: In 2004, a school population was 1250. By 2012 the population had dropped to 875. Assume the population is changing linearly. ⓐ How much did the population drop between the year 2004 and 2012? ⓑ What is the average population decline per year? ⓒ Find an equation for the population, P , of the school t years after 2004.
Answer: a) 375 ; b) dropped an average of 46.875, or about 47 people per year ; c) $$y = - 46.875 t + 1250$$
